mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of sorting?

options.text: To arrange elements in a specific order based on a comparison criterion
To randomly shuffle elements in a list
To count the number of elements in a dataset
To remove duplicate elements from a list

mcqs.explanation: Sorting is the process of arranging elements in a specific order (ascending or descending) based on a comparison criterion, making data easier to search and analyze.

mcqs.question: Which sorting algorithm has a lower bound of Ω(n log n) for comparison-based sorts?
mcqs.explanation: Comparison-based sorting algorithms like Merge Sort have a theoretical lower bound of Ω(n log n) for their time complexity.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of an in-place sorting algorithm?
mcqs.explanation: Quick Sort is an in-place sorting algorithm because it requires only O(1) additional space (excluding recursion stack space).

mcqs.question: What does it mean for a sorting algorithm to be stable?

options.text: It runs in O(n) time complexity
It maintains the relative order of equal elements
It is always faster than unstable sorts

mcqs.explanation: A stable sorting algorithm preserves the relative order of equal elements, which is important in certain applications like sorting database records.

mcqs.question: Which sorting algorithm is adaptive and performs better on nearly sorted data?
mcqs.explanation: Insertion Sort is adaptive because it performs better (closer to O(n)) when the input data is nearly sorted.
